236	@app.post("/v1/chat/completions")
237	async def request_chat_completion(request: ChatCompletionRequest, raw_request: fastapi.Request):
238	    """OpenAI-compatible chat completion API.
239	    API reference: https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat
240	    """
241	    # - Check the requested model.
242	    server_context: ServerContext = ServerContext.current()
243	    request_final_usage_include_extra = server_context.enable_debug
244	    request_include_debug_config = server_context.enable_debug
245	
246	    if not request_include_debug_config:
247	        request.debug_config = None
248	
249	    async_engine = server_context.get_engine(request.model)
250	    if async_engine is None:
251	        return error_protocol.create_error_response(
252	            HTTPStatus.BAD_REQUEST,
253	            message=f'The requested model "{request.model}" is not served.',
254	        )
255	    # FIXME: This is a temporary solution to make sure
256	    # prep_recv, remote_send and start_generation process the same request
257	    request_id = (
258	        request.user if request.user is not None else f"chatcmpl-{engine_utils.random_uuid()}"
259	    )
260	
261	    # Streaming response.
262	    if request.stream:
263	        # We manually get the first response from generator to
264	        # capture potential exceptions in this scope, rather then
265	        # the StreamingResponse scope.
266	        stream_generator = async_engine._handle_chat_completion(
267	            request,
268	            request_id,
269	            request_final_usage_include_extra=request_final_usage_include_extra,
270	        )
271	        first_response = await anext(  # noqa: F821
272	            stream_generator
273	        )
274	
275	        async def completion_stream_generator() -> AsyncGenerator[str, None]:
276	            if isinstance(first_response, StopAsyncIteration):
277	                yield "data: [DONE]\n\n"
278	                return
279	            yield f"data: {first_response.model_dump_json(by_alias=True)}\n\n"
280	            async for response in stream_generator:
281	                yield f"data: {response.model_dump_json(by_alias=True)}\n\n"
282	            yield "data: [DONE]\n\n"
283	
284	        return fastapi.responses.StreamingResponse(
285	            completion_stream_generator(), media_type="text/event-stream"
286	        )
287	
288	    # Normal response.
289	    request_final_usage = None
290	    output_texts = ["" for _ in range(request.n)]
291	    finish_reasons: List[Optional[str]] = [None for _ in range(request.n)]  # noqa: UP006
292	    logprob_results: Optional[List[List[LogProbsContent]]] = (  # noqa: UP006
293	        [[] for _ in range(request.n)] if request.logprobs else None
294	    )
295	
296	    async for response in async_engine._handle_chat_completion(
297	        request,
298	        request_id,
299	        request_final_usage_include_extra=request_final_usage_include_extra,
300	    ):
301	        if await raw_request.is_disconnected():
302	            # In non-streaming cases, the engine will not be notified
303	            # when the request is disconnected.
304	            # Therefore, we check if it is disconnected each time,
305	            # no need to explicitly abort, as the chat completion
306	            # return will trigger abort call
307	            return error_protocol.create_error_response(
308	                HTTPStatus.BAD_REQUEST, message="The request has disconnected"
309	            )
310	        # usage is always the last chunk
311	        if response.usage is not None:
312	            request_final_usage = response.usage
313	            # remove extra information if debug is not enabled
314	            if not server_context.enable_debug:
315	                request_final_usage.extra = None
316	
317	        for choice in response.choices:
318	            assert isinstance(choice.delta.content, str)
319	            output_texts[choice.index] += choice.delta.content
320	            if choice.finish_reason is not None and finish_reasons[choice.index] is None:
321	                finish_reasons[choice.index] = choice.finish_reason
322	            if choice.logprobs is not None:
323	                assert logprob_results is not None
324	                logprob_results[choice.index] += choice.logprobs.content
325	
326	    assert all(finish_reason is not None for finish_reason in finish_reasons)
327	    use_function_calling, tool_calls_list = engine_base.process_function_call_output(
328	        output_texts, finish_reasons
329	    )
330	
331	    return engine_base.wrap_chat_completion_response(
332	        request_id=request_id,
333	        model=request.model,
334	        output_texts=output_texts,
335	        finish_reasons=finish_reasons,
336	        tool_calls_list=tool_calls_list,
337	        logprob_results=logprob_results,
338	        use_function_calling=use_function_calling,
339	        usage=request_final_usage,
340	    )

1	"""Python entrypoint of serve."""
2	
3	from typing import Any, List, Literal, Optional, Tuple, Union  # noqa: UP035
4	
5	import fastapi
6	import uvicorn
7	from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
8	
9	from mlc_llm.protocol import error_protocol
10	from mlc_llm.serve import engine
11	from mlc_llm.serve.embedding_engine import AsyncEmbeddingEngine
12	from mlc_llm.serve.entrypoints import (
13	    debug_entrypoints,
14	    metrics_entrypoints,
15	    microserving_entrypoints,
16	    openai_entrypoints,
17	)
18	from mlc_llm.serve.server import ServerContext
19	from mlc_llm.support import logging
20	
21	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
22	
23	
24	def serve(
25	    model: str,
26	    device: str,
27	    model_lib: Optional[str],
28	    mode: Literal["local", "interactive", "server"],
29	    enable_debug: bool,
30	    additional_models: List[Union[str, Tuple[str, str]]],  # noqa: UP006
31	    embedding_model: Optional[str],
32	    embedding_model_lib: Optional[str],
33	    tensor_parallel_shards: Optional[int],
34	    pipeline_parallel_stages: Optional[int],
35	    opt: Optional[str],
36	    max_num_sequence: Optional[int],
37	    max_total_sequence_length: Optional[int],
38	    max_single_sequence_length: Optional[int],
39	    prefill_chunk_size: Optional[int],
40	    sliding_window_size: Optional[int],
41	    attention_sink_size: Optional[int],
42	    max_history_size: Optional[int],
43	    gpu_memory_utilization: Optional[float],
44	    speculative_mode: Literal["disable", "small_draft", "eagle", "medusa"],
45	    spec_draft_length: Optional[int],
46	    spec_tree_width: Optional[int],
47	    prefix_cache_mode: Literal["disable", "radix"],
48	    prefix_cache_max_num_recycling_seqs: Optional[int],
49	    prefill_mode: Literal["hybrid", "chunked"],
50	    enable_tracing: bool,
51	    host: str,
52	    port: int,
53	    allow_credentials: bool,
54	    allow_origins: Any,
55	    allow_methods: Any,
56	    allow_headers: Any,
57	    api_key: Optional[str] = None,
58	):
59	    """Serve the model with the specified configuration."""
60	    # Create engine and start the background loop
61	    async_engine = engine.AsyncMLCEngine(
62	        model=model,
63	        device=device,
64	        model_lib=model_lib,
65	        mode=mode,
66	        engine_config=engine.EngineConfig(
67	            additional_models=additional_models,
68	            tensor_parallel_shards=tensor_parallel_shards,
69	            pipeline_parallel_stages=pipeline_parallel_stages,
70	            opt=opt,
71	            max_num_sequence=max_num_sequence,
72	            max_total_sequence_length=max_total_sequence_length,
73	            max_single_sequence_length=max_single_sequence_length,
74	            prefill_chunk_size=prefill_chunk_size,
75	            sliding_window_size=sliding_window_size,
76	            attention_sink_size=attention_sink_size,
77	            max_history_size=max_history_size,
78	            gpu_memory_utilization=gpu_memory_utilization,
79	            speculative_mode=speculative_mode,
80	            spec_draft_length=spec_draft_length,
81	            spec_tree_width=spec_tree_width,
82	            prefix_cache_mode=prefix_cache_mode,
83	            prefix_cache_max_num_recycling_seqs=prefix_cache_max_num_recycling_seqs,
84	            prefill_mode=prefill_mode,
85	        ),
86	        enable_tracing=enable_tracing,
87	    )
88	
89	    # Set up embedding model if specified
90	    emb_engine = None
91	    if embedding_model is not None:
92	        if embedding_model_lib is None:
93	            raise ValueError(
94	                "--embedding-model-lib is required when --embedding-model is specified."
95	            )
96	        emb_engine = AsyncEmbeddingEngine(
97	            model=embedding_model,
98	            model_lib=embedding_model_lib,
99	            device=device,
100	        )
101	        logger.info("Embedding model %s loaded successfully.", embedding_model)
102	
103	    with ServerContext() as server_context:
104	        server_context.add_model(model, async_engine)
105	        if emb_engine is not None:
106	            server_context.add_embedding_engine(embedding_model, emb_engine)
107	        server_context.api_key = api_key
108	
109	        app = fastapi.FastAPI()
110	        app.add_middleware(
111	            CORSMiddleware,
112	            allow_credentials=allow_credentials,
113	            allow_origins=allow_origins,
114	            allow_methods=allow_methods,
115	            allow_headers=allow_headers,
116	        )
117	
118	        app.include_router(openai_entrypoints.app)
119	        app.include_router(metrics_entrypoints.app)
120	        app.include_router(microserving_entrypoints.app)
121	
122	        server_context.enable_debug = enable_debug
123	
124	        if enable_debug:
125	            app.include_router(debug_entrypoints.app)
126	            logger.info("Enable debug endpoint and debug_config in requests...")
127	
128	        app.exception_handler(error_protocol.BadRequestError)(
129	            error_protocol.bad_request_error_handler
130	        )
131	        uvicorn.run(app, host=host, port=port, log_level="info")

258	class ChatCompletionRequest(BaseModel):
259	    """OpenAI chat completion request protocol.
260	    API reference: https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create
261	    """
262	
263	    messages: List[ChatCompletionMessage]  # noqa: UP006
264	    model: Optional[str] = None
265	    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None
266	    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None
267	    logprobs: bool = False
268	    top_logprobs: int = 0
269	    logit_bias: Optional[Dict[int, float]] = None  # noqa: UP006
270	    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None
271	    n: int = 1
272	    seed: Optional[int] = None
273	    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None  # noqa: UP006
274	    stream: bool = False
275	    stream_options: Optional[StreamOptions] = None
276	    temperature: Optional[float] = None
277	    top_p: Optional[float] = None
278	    tools: Optional[List[ChatTool]] = None  # noqa: UP006
279	    tool_choice: Optional[Union[Literal["none", "auto"], Dict]] = None  # noqa: UP006
280	    user: Optional[str] = None
281	    response_format: Optional[RequestResponseFormat] = None
282	    # NOTE: debug_config is not part of OpenAI protocol
283	    # we add it to enable extra debug options
284	    debug_config: Optional[DebugConfig] = None

286	    @field_validator("frequency_penalty", "presence_penalty")
287	    @classmethod
288	    def check_penalty_range(cls, penalty_value: Optional[float]) -> Optional[float]:
289	        """Check if the penalty value is in range [-2, 2]."""
290	        if penalty_value and (penalty_value < -2 or penalty_value > 2):
291	            raise ValueError("Penalty value should be in range [-2, 2].")
292	        return penalty_value
293	
294	    @field_validator("logit_bias")
295	    @classmethod
296	    def check_logit_bias(
297	        cls,
298	        logit_bias_value: Optional[Dict[int, float]],  # noqa: UP006
299	    ) -> Optional[Dict[int, float]]:  # noqa: UP006
300	        """Check if the logit bias key is given as an integer."""
301	        if logit_bias_value is None:
302	            return None
303	        for token_id, bias in logit_bias_value.items():
304	            if abs(bias) > 100:
305	                raise ValueError(
306	                    "Logit bias value should be in range [-100, 100], while value "
307	                    f"{bias} is given for token id {token_id}"
308	                )
309	        return logit_bias_value
310	
311	    @model_validator(mode="after")
312	    def check_logprobs(self) -> "ChatCompletionRequest":
313	        """Check if the logprobs requirements are valid."""
314	        if self.top_logprobs < 0 or self.top_logprobs > CHAT_COMPLETION_MAX_TOP_LOGPROBS:
315	            raise ValueError(
316	                f'"top_logprobs" must be in range [0, {CHAT_COMPLETION_MAX_TOP_LOGPROBS}]'
317	            )
318	        if not self.logprobs and self.top_logprobs > 0:
319	            raise ValueError('"logprobs" must be True to support "top_logprobs"')
320	        return self
321	
322	    @model_validator(mode="after")
323	    def check_stream_options(self) -> "ChatCompletionRequest":
324	        """Check stream options"""
325	        if self.stream_options is None:
326	            return self
327	        if not self.stream:
328	            raise ValueError("stream must be set to True when stream_options is present")
329	        return self
330	
331	    @model_validator(mode="after")
332	    def check_debug_config(self) -> "ChatCompletionRequest":
333	        """Check debug config"""
334	        if self.debug_config is None:
335	            return self
336	
337	        if self.debug_config.special_request is None:
338	            return self
339	
340	        if not self.stream:
341	            raise ValueError("DebugConfig.special_request requires stream=True")
342	
343	        if self.stream_options is None or not self.stream_options.include_usage:
344	            raise ValueError("DebugConfig.special_request requires include_usage in stream_options")
345	
346	        return self
347	
348	    def check_message_validity(self) -> None:
349	        """Check if the given chat messages are valid. Return error message if invalid."""
350	        for i, message in enumerate(self.messages):
351	            if message.role == "system" and i != 0:
352	                raise BadRequestError(
353	                    f"System prompt at position {i} in the message list is invalid."
354	                )
355	            if message.tool_call_id is not None:
356	                if message.role != "tool":
357	                    raise BadRequestError("Non-tool message having `tool_call_id` is invalid.")
358	            if isinstance(message.content, list):
359	                if message.role != "user":
360	                    raise BadRequestError("Non-user message having a list of content is invalid.")
361	            if message.tool_calls is not None:
362	                if message.role != "assistant":
363	                    raise BadRequestError("Non-assistant message having `tool_calls` is invalid.")
364	                raise BadRequestError("Assistant message having `tool_calls` is not supported yet.")
365	
366	    def check_function_call_usage(self, conv_template: Conversation) -> None:
367	        """Check if function calling is used and update the conversation template.
368	        Return error message if invalid request format for function calling.
369	        """
370	
371	        # return if no tools are provided or tool_choice is set to none
372	        if self.tools is None or (isinstance(self.tool_choice, str) and self.tool_choice == "none"):
373	            conv_template.use_function_calling = False
374	            return
375	
376	        # select the tool based on the tool_choice if specified
377	        if isinstance(self.tool_choice, dict):
378	            if self.tool_choice["type"] != "function":
379	                raise BadRequestError("Only 'function' tool choice is supported")
380	
381	            if len(self.tool_choice["function"]) > 1:
382	                raise BadRequestError("Only one tool is supported when tool_choice is specified")
383	
384	            for tool in self.tools:
385	                if tool.function.name == self.tool_choice["function"]["name"]:
386	                    conv_template.use_function_calling = True
387	                    conv_template.function_string = tool.function.model_dump_json(by_alias=True)
388	                    return
389	
390	            raise BadRequestError(
391	                f"The tool_choice function {self.tool_choice['function']['name']}"
392	                " is not found in the tools list"
393	            )
394	
395	        if isinstance(self.tool_choice, str) and self.tool_choice != "auto":
396	            raise BadRequestError(f"Invalid tool_choice value: {self.tool_choice}")
397	
398	        function_list = []
399	        for tool in self.tools:
400	            if tool.type != "function":
401	                raise BadRequestError("Only 'function' tool type is supported")
402	            function_list.append(tool.function.model_dump(by_alias=True))
403	
404	        conv_template.use_function_calling = True
405	        conv_template.function_string = json.dumps(function_list)
406	
407

439	    """

450	def openai_api_get_unsupported_fields(
451	    request: Union[CompletionRequest, ChatCompletionRequest],
452	) -> List[str]:  # noqa: UP006
453	    """Get the unsupported fields in the request."""
454	    unsupported_field_default_values: List[Tuple[str, Any]] = [  # noqa: UP006
455	        ("best_of", 1),
456	    ]
457	
458	    unsupported_fields: List[str] = []  # noqa: UP006
459	    for field, value in unsupported_field_default_values:
460	        if hasattr(request, field) and getattr(request, field) != value:
461	            unsupported_fields.append(field)
462	    return unsupported_fields

1	"""The no quantization config"""
2	
3	from dataclasses import dataclass
4	
5	
6	@dataclass
7	class NoQuantize:
8	    """Configuration for no quantization"""
9	
10	    name: str

1	"""AWQ Quantization"""
2	
3	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
4	from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Optional  # noqa: UP035
5	
6	from tvm import DataType, DataTypeCode, te, tirx, topi
7	from tvm.relax.frontend import nn
8	from tvm.runtime import Tensor
9	
10	from mlc_llm.loader import QuantizeMapping
11	
12	from .utils import convert_uint_to_float, is_final_fc, is_moe_gate
13	
14	
15	def _make_divisible(c, divisor):
16	    return (c + divisor - 1) // divisor
17	
18	
19	def _calculate_zeros_width(in_features, group_size=128, pack_num=8):
20	    if group_size >= 128:
21	        size_multiplier = 1
22	    elif group_size == 64:
23	        size_multiplier = 2
24	    elif group_size == 32:
25	        size_multiplier = 4
26	    else:
27	        raise NotImplementedError
28	
29	    base_width = _make_divisible(in_features // group_size, pack_num)
30	    base_width = _make_divisible(base_width, size_multiplier) * size_multiplier
31	    return base_width
32	
33	
34	@dataclass
35	class AWQQuantize:
36	    """Configuration for AWQ quantization"""
37	
38	    name: str
39	    kind: str
40	    group_size: int
41	    quantize_dtype: str  # "int3", "int4", "int8"
42	    storage_dtype: str  # "uint32"
43	    model_dtype: str  # "float16", "float32"
44	
45	    num_elem_per_storage: int = 0
46	    num_storage_per_group: int = 0
47	    max_int_value: int = 0
48	
49	    prebuilt_quantize_func: Dict[str, Callable[[Tensor], Tensor]] = field(  # noqa: UP006
50	        default_factory=lambda: {}
51	    )
52	
53	    def __post_init__(self):
54	        assert self.kind == "awq"
55	        quantize_dtype = DataType(self.quantize_dtype)
56	        storage_dtype = DataType(self.storage_dtype)
57	        model_dtype = DataType(self.model_dtype)
58	        assert quantize_dtype.type_code == DataTypeCode.INT
59	        assert storage_dtype.type_code == DataTypeCode.UINT
60	        assert model_dtype.type_code == DataTypeCode.FLOAT
61	        if storage_dtype.bits < quantize_dtype.bits:
62	            raise ValueError("Storage unit should be greater or equal to quantized element")
63	
64	        self.num_elem_per_storage = storage_dtype.bits // quantize_dtype.bits
65	        if self.group_size % self.num_elem_per_storage != 0:
66	            raise ValueError("Group size should be divisible by numbers of elements per storage")
67	        self.num_storage_per_group = self.group_size // self.num_elem_per_storage
68	        self.max_int_value = (2 ** (quantize_dtype.bits - 1)) - 1
69	
70	    def quantize_model(
71	        self,
72	        model: nn.Module,
73	        quant_map: QuantizeMapping,
74	        name_prefix: str,
75	    ) -> nn.Module:
76	        """
77	        Quantize model with awq quantization.
78	
79	        Parameters
80	        ----------
81	        model : nn.Module
82	            The non-quantized nn.Module.
83	
84	        quant_map : QuantizeMapping
85	            The quantize mapping with name mapping and func mapping.
86	
87	        name_prefix : str
88	            The name prefix for visited weight.
89	
90	        Returns
91	        -------
92	        ret : nn.Module
93	            The quantized nn.Module.
94	        """
95	
96	        class _Mutator(nn.Mutator):
97	            def __init__(self, config: AWQQuantize, quant_map: QuantizeMapping) -> None:
98	                super().__init__()
99	                self.config = config
100	                self.quant_map = quant_map
101	
102	            def visit_module(self, name: str, node: nn.Module) -> Any:
103	                """
104	                The visiting method for awq quantization of nn.Module nodes.
105	
106	                Parameters
107	                ----------
108	                name : str
109	                    The name of the current node
110	
111	                node : nn.Module
112	                    The current node of nn.Module to mutate.
113	
114	                Returns
115	                -------
116	                ret_node : Any
117	                    The new node to replace current node.
118	                """
119	
120	                if (
121	                    isinstance(node, nn.Linear)
122	                    and not is_final_fc(name)
123	                    and not is_moe_gate(name, node)
124	                ):
125	                    return AWQQuantizeLinear.from_linear(node, self.config)
126	                return self.visit(name, node)
127	
128	        model.to(dtype=self.model_dtype)
129	        mutator = _Mutator(self, quant_map)
130	        model = mutator.visit(name_prefix, model)
131	        return model
132	
133	    def _dequantize(
134	        self,
135	        weight: te.Tensor,
136	        zeros: te.Tensor,
137	        scale: te.Tensor,
138	        out_shape: Optional[List[tirx.Expr]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
139	    ):
140	        float_weight = convert_uint_to_float(
141	            weight,
142	            DataType(self.quantize_dtype).bits,
143	            self.num_elem_per_storage,
144	            self.storage_dtype,
145	            self.model_dtype,
146	            out_shape=[weight.shape[0], weight.shape[1] * self.num_elem_per_storage],
147	            ft_reorder=True,
148	        )
149	        float_zeros = convert_uint_to_float(
150	            zeros,
151	            DataType(self.quantize_dtype).bits,
152	            self.num_elem_per_storage,
153	            self.storage_dtype,
154	            self.model_dtype,
155	            out_shape=[zeros.shape[0], zeros.shape[1] * self.num_elem_per_storage],
156	            ft_reorder=True,
157	        )
158	        float_weight = topi.transpose(float_weight)
159	        float_zeros = topi.transpose(float_zeros)
160	        scale = topi.transpose(scale)
161	        return te.compute(
162	            shape=(
163	                [weight.shape[0], weight.shape[1] * self.num_elem_per_storage]
164	                if out_shape is None
165	                else out_shape
166	            ),
167	            fcompute=lambda i, j: tirx.Mul(
168	                tirx.Sub(float_weight[i, j], float_zeros[i, j // self.group_size]),
169	                scale[i, j // self.group_size],
170	            ),
171	            name="dequantize",
172	        )

175	class AWQQuantizeLinear(nn.Module):
176	    """An nn.Linear module with AWQ quantization"""
177	
178	    def __init__(
179	        self,
180	        in_features: int,
181	        out_features: int,
182	        config: AWQQuantize,
183	        bias: bool = True,
184	        out_dtype: Optional[str] = None,
185	    ) -> None:
186	        super().__init__()
187	        self.in_features = in_features
188	        self.out_features = out_features
189	        self.out_dtype = out_dtype
190	        self.config = config
191	        self.qweight = nn.Parameter(
192	            (in_features, out_features // config.num_elem_per_storage),
193	            config.storage_dtype,
194	        )
195	        self.qzeros = nn.Parameter(
196	            (
197	                in_features // config.group_size,
198	                out_features // config.num_elem_per_storage,
199	            ),
200	            config.storage_dtype,
201	        )
202	        self.scales = nn.Parameter(
203	            (in_features // config.group_size, out_features), config.model_dtype
204	        )
205	        if bias:
206	            self.bias = nn.Parameter(
207	                (out_features,), config.model_dtype if out_dtype is None else out_dtype
208	            )
209	        else:
210	            self.bias = None
211	
212	    @staticmethod
213	    def from_linear(linear: nn.Linear, config: AWQQuantize) -> "AWQQuantizeLinear":
214	        """
215	        Converts a non-quantized nn.Linear to a group quantized AWQQuantizeLinear
216	
217	        Parameters
218	        ----------
219	        linear : nn.Linear
220	            The non-quantized nn.Linear.
221	
222	        config : AWQQuantize
223	            The awq quantization config.
224	
225	        Returns
226	        -------
227	        ret : GroupQuantizeLinear
228	            The awq quantized AWQQuantizeLinear layer.
229	        """
230	        return AWQQuantizeLinear(
231	            in_features=linear.in_features,
232	            out_features=linear.out_features,
233	            config=config,
234	            bias=getattr(linear, "bias", None) is not None,
235	            out_dtype=linear.out_dtype,
236	        )
237	
238	    def forward(self, x: nn.Tensor) -> nn.Tensor:
239	        """
240	        Forward method for awq quantized linear layer
241	
242	        Parameters
243	        ----------
244	        x : nn.Tensor
245	            The input tensor.
246	
247	        Returns
248	        -------
249	        ret : nn.Tensor
250	            The output tensor for the group quantized linear layer.
251	        """
252	        w = nn.op.tensor_expr_op(
253	            lambda weight, zeros, scale: self.config._dequantize(
254	                weight,
255	                zeros,
256	                scale,
257	                [
258	                    tirx.IntImm("int64", self.out_features),
259	                    tirx.IntImm("int64", self.in_features),
260	                ],
261	            ),
262	            name_hint="dequantize",
263	            args=[self.qweight, self.qzeros, self.scales],
264	        )
265	        w = nn.op.permute_dims(w)
266	        x = nn.op.matmul(x, w, out_dtype=self.out_dtype)
267	        if self.bias is not None:
268	            x = x + self.bias
269	        return x
270	
271	    def to(self, dtype: Optional[str] = None) -> None:
272	        """
273	        Override to() such that we do not convert bias if there is an out_dtype.
274	        Otherwise, we might run into dtype mismatch when computing x + self.bias.
275	        """
276	        self.qweight.to(dtype=dtype)
277	        self.qzeros.to(dtype=dtype)
278	        self.scales.to(dtype=dtype)
279	        if self.bias is not None and self.out_dtype is None:
280	            self.bias.to(dtype=dtype)
281	        if dtype is not None and isinstance(getattr(self, "dtype", None), str):
282	            self.dtype = dtype

30	@dataclass
31	class PerTensorQuantize:
32	    """Configuration for per-tensor quantization"""
33	
34	    name: str
35	    kind: str
36	    activation_dtype: Literal["float8_e4m3fn", "float8_e5m2"]
37	    weight_dtype: Literal["float8_e4m3fn", "float8_e5m2"]
38	    storage_dtype: Literal["uint32", "float8_e4m3fn", "float8_e5m2"]
39	    model_dtype: Literal["float16"]
40	    quantize_embedding: bool = True
41	    quantize_final_fc: bool = True
42	    quantize_linear: bool = True
43	
44	    num_elem_per_storage: int = 0
45	    max_int_value: int = 0
46	    use_scale: bool = True
47	    # The calibration mode for quantization. If set to "inference", the model is built for
48	    # inference. This should be used after calibration is done.
49	    # If set to "max", the model is built for calibration that computes the scale using max value of
50	    # the activations.
51	    calibration_mode: Literal["inference", "max"] = "inference"
52	    tensor_parallel_shards: int = 1
53	
54	    def __post_init__(self):
55	        assert self.kind == "per-tensor-quant"
56	        self.num_elem_per_storage = (
57	            DataType(self.storage_dtype).bits // DataType(self.weight_dtype).bits
58	        )
59	        self.max_int_value = int(tirx.max_value(self.weight_dtype).value)
60	        self._quantize_func_cache = {}
61	
62	    def quantize_model(
63	        self,
64	        model: nn.Module,
65	        quant_map: QuantizeMapping,
66	        name_prefix: str,
67	        tensor_parallel_shards: int,
68	    ) -> nn.Module:
69	        """
70	        Quantize model with per-tensor quantization
71	
72	        Parameters
73	        ----------
74	        model : nn.Module
75	            The non-quantized nn.Module.
76	
77	        quant_map : QuantizeMapping
78	            The quantize mapping with name mapping and func mapping.
79	
80	        name_prefix : str
81	            The name prefix for visited weight.
82	
83	        tensor_parallel_shards : int
84	            The number of tensor parallel shards.
85	
86	        Returns
87	        -------
88	        ret : nn.Module
89	            The quantized nn.Module.
90	        """
91	
92	        self.tensor_parallel_shards = tensor_parallel_shards
93	
94	        class _Mutator(nn.Mutator):
95	            def __init__(self, config: PerTensorQuantize, quant_map: QuantizeMapping) -> None:
96	                super().__init__()
97	                self.config = config
98	                self.quant_map = quant_map
99	
100	            def visit_module(self, name: str, node: nn.Module) -> Any:
101	                """
102	                The visiting method for per-tensor quantization of nn.Module nodes.
103	
104	                Parameters
105	                ----------
106	                name : str
107	                    The name of the current node.
108	
109	                node : nn.Module
110	                    The current node of nn.Module to mutate.
111	
112	                Returns
113	                ------
114	                ret_node: Any
115	                    The new node to replace current node.
116	                """
117	                weight_name = f"{name}.weight"
118	                param_names = (
119	                    [f"{name}.q_weight", f"{name}.q_scale"]
120	                    if self.config.use_scale
121	                    else [
122	                        f"{name}.q_weight",
123	                    ]
124	                )
125	                if (
126	                    isinstance(node, nn.Linear)
127	                    and self.config.quantize_linear
128	                    and (not is_final_fc(name) or self.config.quantize_final_fc)
129	                    and not is_moe_gate(name, node)
130	                ):
131	                    self.quant_map.param_map[weight_name] = param_names
132	                    self.quant_map.map_func[weight_name] = self.config.quantize_weight
133	                    op = PerTensorQuantizeLinear.from_linear(node, self.config, name)
134	                elif isinstance(node, nn.Embedding) and self.config.quantize_embedding:
135	                    self.quant_map.param_map[weight_name] = param_names
136	                    self.quant_map.map_func[weight_name] = self.config.quantize_weight
137	                    op = PerTensorQuantizeEmbedding.from_embedding(node, self.config)
138	                elif isinstance(node, MixtralExperts):
139	                    self.quant_map.param_map[weight_name] = param_names
140	                    self.quant_map.map_func[weight_name] = self.config.quantize_weight
141	                    op = PerTensorQuantizeMixtralExperts.from_mixtral_experts(
142	                        node, self.config, name
143	                    )
144	                else:
145	                    return self.visit(name, node)
146	
147	                if hasattr(op, "q_calibration_scale") and op.q_calibration_scale:
148	                    # update quant_map for calibration scale
149	                    param_name = f"{name}.q_calibration_scale"
150	                    old_map_func = self.quant_map.map_func[weight_name]
151	
152	                    def map_func(*args, **kwargs):
153	                        # placeholder for calibration scale, the actual value will be set after
154	                        # calibration.
155	                        scale = runtime.empty(
156	                            shape=op.q_calibration_scale.shape,
157	                            dtype=op.q_calibration_scale.dtype,
158	                        )
159	                        return [*old_map_func(*args, **kwargs), scale]
160	
161	                    self.quant_map.param_map[weight_name].append(param_name)
162	                    self.quant_map.map_func[weight_name] = map_func
163	                return op
164	
165	        model.to(dtype=self.model_dtype)
166	        mutator = _Mutator(self, quant_map)
167	        model = mutator.visit(name_prefix, model)
168	        return model
169	
170	    def quantize_weight(self, weight) -> List[Tensor]:  # noqa: UP006
171	        """
172	        Quantize weight with per-tensor quantization.
173	
174	        Parameters
175	        ----------
176	        weight : Tensor
177	            The weight to quantize.
178	
179	        Returns
180	        -------
181	        ret : List[Tensor]
182	            The quantized weight and the scale if use_scale is True.
183	        """
184	        device = weight.device
185	        device_type = device._DEVICE_TYPE_TO_NAME[device.dlpack_device_type()]
186	
187	        def _create_quantize_func() -> IRModule:
188	            if DataType(self.weight_dtype).type_code in [
189	                DataTypeCode.Float8E4M3FN,
190	                DataTypeCode.Float8E5M2,
191	            ]:
192	                quantize_func = functools.partial(
193	                    self.quantize_float8,
194	                    quantize_dtype=self.weight_dtype,
195	                    storage_dtype=self.storage_dtype,
196	                )
197	            else:
198	                assert NotImplementedError()
199	
200	            class Quantizer(nn.Module):
201	                """Quantizer module for per-tensor quantization."""
202	
203	                def main(self, weight: nn.Tensor):
204	                    return quantize_func(weight)
205	
206	            mod = Quantizer()
207	            mod, _ = mod.export_tvm(
208	                spec={"main": {"weight": nn.spec.Tensor(weight.shape, weight.dtype)}}
209	            )
210	            return mod
211	
212	        key = f"({weight.shape}, {weight.dtype}, {device_type}"
213	        quantize_func = self._quantize_func_cache.get(key, None)
214	        if quantize_func is None:
215	            logger.info("Compiling quantize function for key: %s", key)
216	            quantize_func = compile_quantize_func(_create_quantize_func(), device)
217	            self._quantize_func_cache[key] = quantize_func
218	        return quantize_func(weight)
219	
220	    def quantize_float8(
221	        self,
222	        tensor: nn.Tensor,
223	        quantize_dtype: str,
224	        storage_dtype: str,
225	    ) -> Union[Tuple[nn.Tensor], Tuple[nn.Tensor, nn.Tensor]]:  # noqa: UP006
226	        """Per-tensor quantization for weight tensor, defined in tensor expression."""
227	
228	        if self.use_scale:
229	            # min_scaling_factor taken from TRT-LLM
230	            def _compute_scale(x: te.Tensor) -> te.Tensor:
231	                max_abs = topi.max(topi.abs(x))
232	                min_scaling_factor = tirx.const(
233	                    1.0 / (self.max_int_value * 512.0), self.model_dtype
234	                )
235	                scale = topi.maximum(
236	                    max_abs.astype(self.model_dtype) / self.max_int_value,
237	                    min_scaling_factor,
238	                ).astype("float32")
239	                scale = topi.expand_dims(scale, axis=0)
240	                return scale
241	
242	            scale = nn.tensor_expr_op(_compute_scale, "compute_scale", args=[tensor])
243	        else:
244	            scale = None
245	
246	        def _compute_quantized_tensor(weight: te.Tensor, scale: Optional[te.Tensor]) -> te.Tensor:
247	            elem_storage_dtype = (
248	                f"uint{DataType(quantize_dtype).bits}"
249	                if DataType(storage_dtype).type_code == DataTypeCode.UINT
250	                else quantize_dtype
251	            )
252	            scaled_tensor = te.compute(
253	                shape=weight.shape,
254	                fcompute=lambda *idx: tirx.Cast(
255	                    self.storage_dtype,
256	                    tirx.reinterpret(
257	                        elem_storage_dtype,
258	                        tirx.Cast(
259	                            quantize_dtype,
260	                            weight(*idx) / scale(0) if scale is not None else weight(*idx),
261	                        ),
262	                    ),
263	                ),
264	            )
265	
266	            if quantize_dtype == self.storage_dtype:
267	                return scaled_tensor
268	
269	            packed_weight = pack_weight(
270	                scaled_tensor,
271	                axis=-1,
272	                num_elem_per_storage=self.num_elem_per_storage,
273	                weight_dtype=self.weight_dtype,
274	                storage_dtype=self.storage_dtype,
275	            )
276	
277	            return packed_weight
278	
279	        quantized_tensor = nn.tensor_expr_op(
280	            _compute_quantized_tensor, "compute_quantized_tensor", args=[tensor, scale]
281	        )
282	
283	        if self.use_scale:
284	            return quantized_tensor, scale
285	        return (quantized_tensor,)
286	
287	    def _dequantize(
288	        self,
289	        q_weight: te.Tensor,
290	        scale: Optional[te.Tensor] = None,
291	        out_shape: Optional[Sequence[tirx.Expr]] = None,
292	    ) -> te.Tensor:
293	        if self.use_scale:
294	            assert scale is not None
295	        if DataType(self.weight_dtype).type_code in [
296	            DataTypeCode.Float8E4M3FN,
297	            DataTypeCode.Float8E5M2,
298	        ]:
299	            return self.dequantize_float8(q_weight, scale, self.weight_dtype, out_shape)
300	        raise NotImplementedError()
301	
302	    def dequantize_float8(
303	        self,
304	        q_tensor: te.Tensor,
305	        scale: Optional[te.Tensor],
306	        quantize_dtype: str,
307	        out_shape: Optional[Sequence[tirx.Expr]] = None,
308	    ) -> te.Tensor:
309	        """Dequantize a fp8 tensor (input or weight) to higher-precision float."""
310	        if quantize_dtype != self.storage_dtype:
311	            dequantized_tensor = convert_uint_packed_fp8_to_float(
312	                q_tensor,
313	                self.num_elem_per_storage,
314	                self.storage_dtype,
315	                self.model_dtype,
316	                quantize_dtype,
317	                axis=-1,
318	                out_shape=out_shape,
319	            )
320	        else:
321	            dequantized_tensor = q_tensor.astype(self.model_dtype)
322	        if scale is not None:
323	            dequantized_tensor = dequantized_tensor * scale.astype(dequantized_tensor.dtype)
324	        return dequantized_tensor

327	class PerTensorQuantizeLinear(nn.Module):
328	    """An nn.Linear module with per-tensor quantization."""
329	
330	    def __init__(
331	        self,
332	        in_features: int,
333	        out_features: Union[int, tirx.Var],
334	        config: PerTensorQuantize,
335	        name: str,
336	        bias: bool = True,
337	        out_dtype: Optional[str] = None,
338	    ) -> None:
339	        super().__init__()
340	        self.in_features = in_features
341	        self.out_features = out_features
342	        self.out_dtype = out_dtype or config.model_dtype
343	        self.config = config
344	        self.name = name
345	        self.q_weight = nn.Parameter(
346	            (out_features, tirx.ceildiv(in_features, config.num_elem_per_storage)),
347	            config.storage_dtype,
348	        )
349	        self.q_calibration_scale = None
350	        if config.use_scale:
351	            self.q_scale = nn.Parameter((1,), "float32")
352	            if config.calibration_mode == "inference":
353	                self.q_calibration_scale = nn.Parameter((1,), "float32")
354	        else:
355	            self.q_scale = None
356	        if bias:
357	            self.bias = nn.Parameter(
358	                (out_features,), config.model_dtype if out_dtype is None else out_dtype
359	            )
360	        else:
361	            self.bias = None
362	
363	    @classmethod
364	    def from_linear(
365	        cls, src: nn.Linear, config: PerTensorQuantize, name: str
366	    ) -> "PerTensorQuantizeLinear":
367	        """
368	        Converts a non-quantized nn.Linear to a per-tensor quantized PerTensorQuantizeLinear
369	
370	        Parameters
371	        ----------
372	        src : nn.Linear
373	            The non-quantized nn.Linear.
374	
375	        config : PerTensorQuantize
376	            The per-tensor quantization config.
377	
378	        name: str
379	            The name of the layer.
380	
381	        Returns
382	        -------
383	        ret : PerTensorQuantizeLinear
384	            The per-tensor quantized PerTensorQuantizeLinear layer.
385	        """
386	        out_features, in_features = src.weight.shape
387	        quantized_linear = cls(
388	            in_features=in_features,
389	            out_features=out_features,
390	            config=config,
391	            name=name,
392	            bias=getattr(src, "bias", None) is not None,
393	            out_dtype=src.out_dtype,
394	        )
395	        if quantized_linear.bias is not None:
396	            quantized_linear.bias.attrs = src.bias.attrs
397	        if "shard_strategy" in src.weight.attrs:
398	            shard = src.weight.attrs["shard_strategy"]
399	            apply_sharding(shard, f"{shard.name}_q_weight", quantized_linear.q_weight)
400	            # scale doesn't need to be sharded since it's the same for all shards
401	        return quantized_linear
402	
403	    def forward(self, x: nn.Tensor) -> nn.Tensor:
404	        """
405	        Forward method for per-tensor quantized linear layer.
406	
407	        Parameters
408	        ----------
409	        x : nn.Tensor
410	            The input tensor.
411	
412	        Returns
413	        -------
414	        ret : nn.Tensor
415	            The output tensor for the per-tensor quantized linear layer.
416	        """
417	        # Note: Use calibration scale when calibration is enabled
418	        if self.config.calibration_mode == "inference":
419	            if self.q_calibration_scale:
420	                x /= self.q_calibration_scale.astype(x.dtype)
421	            x_q = x.astype(self.config.activation_dtype)
422	            x_scale = self.q_calibration_scale
423	        elif self.config.calibration_mode == "max":
424	            _, x_scale = self.config.quantize_float8(
425	                x,
426	                quantize_dtype=self.config.activation_dtype,
427	                storage_dtype=self.config.storage_dtype,
428	            )
429	            if self.config.tensor_parallel_shards > 1:
430	                x_scale = nn.ccl_allreduce(x_scale, "max")
431	            x_scale = nn.extern(
432	                "mlc_llm.calibration_observer",
433	                [f"{self.name}.q_calibration_scale", "max", x_scale],
434	                out=nn.Tensor.placeholder(x_scale.shape, x_scale.dtype),
435	            )
436	            x_q = (x / x_scale.astype(x.dtype)).astype(self.config.activation_dtype)
437	            x = x_q.astype(self.config.model_dtype) * x_scale.astype(self.config.model_dtype)
438	        else:
439	            raise ValueError(f"Unknown calibration mode: {self.config.calibration_mode}")
440	
441	        if (
442	            self.config.weight_dtype == self.config.storage_dtype
443	            and self.config.calibration_mode == "inference"
444	        ):
445	            if (
446	                extern.get_store().cutlass_gemm
447	                and functools.reduce(lambda x, y: x * y, x_q.shape[:-1]) != 1
448	            ):
449	                # Dispatch to cutlass kernel for gemm when cutlass is available.
450	                scale = (
451	                    x_scale * self.q_scale
452	                    if self.config.use_scale
453	                    else nn.wrap_nested(
454	                        relax.Constant(runtime.tensor(np.array([1.0]).astype("float32"))),
455	                        "scale",
456	                    )
457	                )
458	                return cutlass.fp8_gemm(
459	                    x_q,
460	                    self.q_weight,
461	                    scale,
462	                    self.config.weight_dtype,
463	                    self.config.model_dtype,
464	                )
465	            x = nn.op.matmul(x_q, nn.permute_dims(self.q_weight), out_dtype="float32")
466	            if self.config.use_scale:
467	                scale = x_scale * self.q_scale
468	                x = x * scale
469	            x = x.astype(self.out_dtype)
470	        else:
471	            w = nn.op.tensor_expr_op(
472	                lambda weight, scale: self.config._dequantize(
473	                    weight,
474	                    scale,
475	                    out_shape=[
476	                        (
477	                            tirx.IntImm("int64", self.out_features)
478	                            if isinstance(self.out_features, int)
479	                            else weight.shape[0]
480	                        ),
481	                        tirx.IntImm("int64", self.in_features),
482	                    ],
483	                ),
484	                "dequantize",
485	                args=[self.q_weight, self.q_scale],
486	            )
487	            x = nn.op.matmul(x, nn.permute_dims(w), out_dtype=self.out_dtype)

489	            x = x + self.bias

492	    def to(self, dtype: Optional[str] = None) -> None:
493	        """
494	        Override to() such that we do not convert bias if there is an out_dtype.
495	        Otherwise, we might run into dtype mismatch when computing x + self.bias.
496	        """
497	        self.q_weight.to(dtype=dtype)
498	        if self.q_scale:
499	            self.q_scale.to(dtype=dtype)
500	        if self.bias is not None and self.out_dtype is None:
501	            self.bias.to(dtype=dtype)
502	        if dtype is not None and isinstance(getattr(self, "dtype", None), str):
503	            self.dtype = dtype

514	            (num, tirx.ceildiv(dim, config.num_elem_per_storage)), config.storage_dtype
515	        )

544	    def forward(self, x: nn.Tensor):
545	        """
546	        Forward method for per-tensor quantized embedding layer.
547	
548	        Parameters
549	        ----------
550	        x : nn.Tensor
551	            The input tensor.
552	
553	        Returns
554	        -------
555	        ret : nn.Tensor
556	            The output tensor for the embedding layer.
557	        """
558	        w = nn.op.tensor_expr_op(
559	            lambda weight, scale: self.config._dequantize(
560	                weight,
561	                scale,
562	                out_shape=[
563	                    (
564	                        tirx.IntImm("int64", self.num)
565	                        if isinstance(self.num, int)
566	                        else weight.shape[0]
567	                    ),
568	                    tirx.IntImm("int64", self.dim),
569	                ],
570	            ),
571	            "dequantize",
572	            args=[self.q_weight, self.q_scale],
573	        )
574	        if x.ndim == 1:
575	            return nn.op.take(w, x, axis=0)
576	        return nn.op.reshape(
577	            nn.op.take(w, nn.op.reshape(x, shape=[-1]), axis=0),
578	            shape=[*x.shape, self.dim],

20	def check_parameter_usage(param_map: "ExternMapping", extern_weights: Set[str]):  # noqa: UP006
21	    """Check that all external parameters have been used and are stored in the weights file."""
22	    used_extern_names = set(functools.reduce(operator.iadd, param_map.param_map.values(), []))
23	    # Check 1. All extern parameters in the weight files are used unless explicitly specified
24	    unused_extern_names = extern_weights - used_extern_names - param_map.unused_params
25	    if unused_extern_names:
26	        logger.warning(
27	            "Unused extern parameters: %s",
28	            ", ".join(sorted(unused_extern_names)),
29	        )
30	    # Check 2. All extern parameters required are stored in the weight files
31	    nonexistent_extern_names = used_extern_names - extern_weights
32	    if nonexistent_extern_names:
33	        raise ValueError(
34	            "The following extern parameters do not exist in the weight files:\n  "
35	            + "\n  ".join(sorted(nonexistent_extern_names)),
36	        )

39	def load_torch_shard(path: Path) -> Iterator[Tuple[str, np.ndarray]]:  # noqa: UP006
40	    """Load and yield PyTorch format parameters."""
41	    import torch
42	
43	    for name, param in torch.load(path, map_location=torch.device("cpu")).items():
44	        if param is None:
45	            logger.warning("Encountered None param, skipping it: %s", name)
46	            continue
47	        param = param.detach().cpu()
48	        dtype = str(param.dtype)
49	        if dtype == "torch.bfloat16":
50	            param = param.float()
51	        param = param.numpy()
52	        yield name, param
53

55	def load_safetensor_shard(path: Path) -> Iterator[Tuple[str, np.ndarray]]:  # noqa: UP006
56	    """Load and yield SafeTensor format parameters."""
57	    import safetensors
58	    import torch
59	
60	    with safetensors.safe_open(path, framework="pt", device="cpu") as in_file:
61	        for name in in_file.keys():
62	            param = in_file.get_tensor(name)
63	            param = param.detach().cpu()
64	            dtype = str(param.dtype)
65	            if dtype == "torch.bfloat16":
66	                import ml_dtypes
67	
68	                param = param.view(torch.float16).cpu().numpy().view(ml_dtypes.bfloat16)
69	            elif dtype == "torch.float8_e4m3fn":
70	                import ml_dtypes
71	
72	                param = param.view(torch.uint8).cpu().numpy().view(ml_dtypes.float8_e4m3fn)
73	            else:
74	                param = param.numpy()

Now I have a comprehensive view of all four subsystems. Let me compile the structural map:

---

## MLC LLM Subsystems Structural Map

### 1. Weight Loader System

**Core Abstraction - `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/loader/loader.py`**
- Simple registry dict `LOADER` mapping loader type strings to loader classes (currently only HuggingFaceLoader)
- `Loader` type alias to `Any` - the loaders are the actual working classes

**Primary Implementation - `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/loader/huggingface_loader.py`**
- `HuggingFaceLoader` class loads PyTorch/SafeTensor format from HuggingFace and converts to MLC parameters
- Takes `ExternMapping` (MLC->HF name mapping) and optional `QuantizeMapping` for on-the-fly quantization
- `load(device, preshard_funcs)` method yields `(name, Tensor)` tuples as an iterator
- Handles file-by-file loading with caching/unloading for memory efficiency
- Supports tensor parallel sharding via optional `preshard_funcs` that split individual params

**Name Mapping - `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/loader/mapping.py`**
- `ExternMapping` dataclass: maps MLC param names to source param names (e.g., `qkv_proj.weight` -> `[q_proj.weight, k_proj.weight, v_proj.weight]`)
- Contains `param_map` (Dict[str, List[str]]) for name correspondence and `map_func` (Dict of transformation functions)
- `QuantizeMapping` dataclass: maps unquantized params to quantized outputs (e.g., `weight` -> `[weight_quantized, weight_scale]`)
- Supports two workflows: on-the-fly quantization during load, or loading pre-quantized external weights

**Standard Loader Helper - `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/loader/standard_loader.py`**
- `make_standard_hf_loader()` factory function that generates loader mappings for common HF patterns
- Handles QKV concatenation, gate+up concatenation (SwiGLU), optional QKV bias, and 1:1 passthrough
- Configurable layer prefixes, name transforms, and num_layers getters

**Statistics Tracking - `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/loader/stats.py`**
- `Stats` dataclass tracks load time, mapping time, quantization time, and memory usage
- Context manager `timer(attr)` for timing code blocks
- `mem_add`/`mem_rm` for tracking RAM usage; logs peak and total bytes loaded

---

### 2. Conversation Templates

**Registry Pattern - `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/conversation_template/registry.py`**
- `ConvTemplateRegistry` class with static `_conv_templates` class variable (Dict[str, Conversation])
- `register_conv_template(conv_template, override=False)` - registers templates by name
- `get_conv_template(name)` - retrieves registered templates; returns None if not found
- Registry populated at module import time (e.g., llama.py calls register on import)

**Protocol Definition - `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/protocol/conversation_protocol.py`**
- `Conversation` Pydantic BaseModel defines the template structure
- Key fields: `system_template`, `system_message`, `roles` (dict of role->prefix strings), `seps` (message separators), `role_content_sep`, `role_empty_sep`, `stop_str`, `stop_token_ids`
- `role_templates` dict defines placeholder replacement patterns per role
- `as_prompt(config)` method converts conversation history to prompt list (supports multimodal with ImageData)
- Handles special cases like Qwen3's reasoning strip and function calling

**Example Template - `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/conversation_template/llama.py`**
- Defines Llama2, Llama3, Llama3.1, Llama4, CodeLlama variants
- Each `ConvTemplateRegistry.register_conv_template()` call instantiates `Conversation` with model-specific format strings
- Shows evolution: Llama2 uses `[INST]`/`[/INST]`, Llama3 uses special header tokens

---

### 3. Quantization System

**Base Registry/Protocol - `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/quantization/quantization.py`**
- `QUANTIZATION` dict maps quantization name strings (e.g., "q4f16_1") to quantization config objects
- `Quantization` type alias defines required interface: `name` (str), `kind` (str), `quantize_model(module) -> module`, `quantize_weight(weight) -> List[Tensor]`

**Quantization Kinds Supported:**
1. **no-quant** (`NoQuantize`): No quantization, just dtype conversion (q0f16, q0bf16, q0f32)
2. **group-quant** (`GroupQuantize`): Group-wise int3/int4/int8 quantization with per-group scales
3. **awq** (`AWQQuantize`): AutoAWQ format with grouped weights, scales, and zeros
4. **ft-quant** (`FTQuantize`): FasterTransformer format, can fallback to group quantization
5. **per-tensor-quant** (`PerTensorQuantize`): FP8 per-tensor quantization (e4m3/e5m2)
6. **block-scale-quant** (`BlockScaleQuantize`): FP8 with block-wise scaling

**Group Quantization Implementation - `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/quantization/group_quantization.py`**
- `GroupQuantize` dataclass: `group_size`, `quantize_dtype`, `storage_dtype`, `model_dtype`, `linear_weight_layout` (KN/NK)
- `quantize_model()` walks the nn.Module via visitor pattern, replacing Linear/Embedding/MixtralExperts with quantized variants
- `quantize_weight()` compiles TVM IRModule for on-the-fly weight quantization
- `GroupQuantizeLinear/Embedding/MixtralExperts` are the quantized layer implementations

**Additional Implementations:**
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/quantization/no_quantization.py`: Simple dtype-only config
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/quantization/awq_quantization.py`: AWQ with 3 outputs (qweight, qzeros, scales)
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/quantization/per_tensor_quantization.py`: FP8 with optional calibration modes
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/quantization/ft_quantization.py`: FasterTransformer compatibility
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/quantization/block_scale_quantization.py`: Block-scale FP8

---

### 4. REST Server + OpenAI Protocol

**Serve Entry Point - `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/interface/serve.py`**
- `serve()` function: main entrypoint that creates `AsyncMLCEngine`, sets up `ServerContext`, builds FastAPI app, runs uvicorn
- Creates `ServerContext` as context manager, adds models/embedding engines to it
- Includes OpenAI router, metrics router, microserving router; optionally debug router
- Configures CORS middleware and exception handlers

**Server Context - `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/serve/server/server_context.py`**
- `ServerContext` class: global singleton storing running models and their `AsyncMLCEngine` instances
- `add_model(hosted_model, engine)` and `get_engine(model)` for model registry
- `add_embedding_engine()` and `get_embedding_engine()` for embedding models
- Context manager `__enter__`/`__exit__` handles engine lifecycle and cleanup

**Popen Server Wrapper - `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/serve/server/popen_server.py`**
- `PopenServer` class: launches server in background subprocess for debugging/testing
- `start()` spawns subprocess, polls until `/v1/models` endpoint is ready
- `terminate()` kills process tree including child processes
- Returns HTTP client URLs: `base_url` and `openai_v1_base_url`

**OpenAI Endpoints - `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/serve/entrypoints/openai_entrypoints.py`**
- `app = fastapi.APIRouter()` with dependencies for API key verification
- `POST /v1/embeddings`: embedding inference with optional dimension truncation
- `GET /v1/models`: list served models
- `POST /v1/completions`: text completion, streaming and non-streaming
- `POST /v1/chat/completions`: chat completion with message history, function calling support
- All endpoints use `async_engine._handle_*_completion()` generators, yielding OpenAI-compatible responses

**OpenAI Protocol - `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/protocol/openai_api_protocol.py`**
- Pydantic models mirroring OpenAI API: `CompletionRequest`, `ChatCompletionRequest`, `EmbeddingRequest`
- Response models: `CompletionResponse`, `ChatCompletionResponse`, `ChatCompletionStreamResponse`
- `ChatCompletionRequest.check_function_call_usage()` validates and sets up function calling in conversation template
- Extends OpenAI protocol with optional `debug_config` field for debugging features

**CLI Serve Command - `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/python/mlc_llm/cli/serve.py`**
- Command-line interface parsing for `mlc_llm serve` command
- `EngineConfigOverride` dataclass for runtime engine configuration overrides
- Arguments include: model, device, model-lib, mode, additional-models, embedding-model, API key, host/port
